common.motto: Dango–the one app for everything DeFi

testnet.title: Message from the founder
testnet.description: Thank you for trying out Dango’s first ever testnet! The purpose of testnet-1 is for testing the basic infrastructure of Dango chain, including consensus, smart contract engine, and indexer. As such, user-facing features are very limited. The coming testnet-2 (expected in June/July) will add our flagship product, the Dango DEX. Then, testnet-3 (August/September) will add margin trading. For now, you can register, open subaccounts, and transfer tokens, that’s it. Upon your first login, you will receive some fake tokens (fake BTC, fake ETH, ...) to play with. Make sure to complete the Galxe quests[ADD LINK] and mint your limited time NFT! g🍡

tpsl.title: TP/SL for Position

faqs.title: FAQs
faqs.question1: How do I unlock a referral link?
faqs.answer1: You must first achieve $10,000 in traded volume. Then, create your referral link by setting the fee share ratio.
faqs.question2: Can I earn points by referring users?
faqs.answer2: Yes. You will get 1 point for every 5 points earned by your direct referrals, and 1 point for every 20 points earned by your indirect referrals.
faqs.question3: Is there a limit to how much of my commission I can share with my referees?
faqs.answer3: Yes. Referrers cannot share more than 50% of their total commission with their referees.
faqs.question4: Why am I not allowed to reduce my fee share ratio?
faqs.answer4: A common "rug pull" is a referrer sets a high fee share ratio to lure referees in, then secretly tune down the ratio. This is to prevent this exact scenario.
faqs.question5: What is the difference between "commission rate" and "fee share ratio"?
faqs.answer5:
Commission rate: how big a portion of trading fee that protocol gives you.
Fee share ratio: how big a portion of the commission you receive, you give to your referees.
faqs.answer5Step1: You trade more than $10k but less than $10M volume. This grants you a 10% commission rate.
faqs.answer5Step2: You set your fee share ratio to 30%.
faqs.answer5Step3: A referee of yours makes a trade, paying $100 in fees.
faqs.answer5Step5: You give $10 × 30% = $3 of that commission to the referee.
faqs.answer5Step6: Net: the referee pays $100 - $3 = $97 in fees; you earn $10 - $3 = $7.
